name: Barbarian Rage: Full Lifecycle (activation + damage bonus + resistance + expiration)
description: Multi-turn Barbarian Rage lifecycle in one scenario (replaces rage.json, rage-ends.json, rage-resistance.json). R1 enters Rage as bonus action and attacks with +2 damage bonus. R2 takes bludgeoning hit from monster (resistance halves damage). R3 ends turn without attacking or taking damage (monster endTurn). R4 rage expires; attack deals normal damage (no +2 bonus). R4 re-enters Rage from remaining pool (3 uses). Tests rage activation, pool decrement, melee damage bonus, B/P/S damage resistance, turn-tracking end condition (rageAttackedThisTurn/rageDamageTakenThisTurn), and pool persistence after rage end.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: Attack twice when you take the Attack action

features.name: Rage
features.description: Enter a Rage as a bonus action. Gain resistance to B/P/S damage and +2 melee damage.

actions.comment: === R1: Enter Rage (bonus) + attack with +2 damage bonus ===
High init to go first.
Enter Rage as bonus action. Pool 3→2.
Rage pool decremented 3→2.
Melee attack WITH rage active — should add +2 rage damage bonus.
15 + 7 = 22 vs AC 5 → hit.
Damage 8 + 4 (STR) + 2 (rage) = 14. Golem 200→186.
Rage damage bonus confirmed: 8+4+2 = 14.
=== R2: End turn (no attack); next R2 monster turn: configure monster to ATTACK — rage resistance halves bludgeoning ===
Switch monster from endTurn to attack so it lands a hit on the raging Barbarian.
Monster attacks with Slam (+20 vs AC 15 — near-guaranteed hit, 1d2+8 = 9-10 bludgeoning). Rage resistance halves to 4-5.
Rage resistance: 9-10 bludgeoning halved to 4-5 → HP 55 - 4/5 = 50-51. Bounded min 49 to cover off-by-one. Without resistance HP would be 45-46.
=== R3: Don't attack, end turn. Monster endTurn. Rage end check: no attack + no damage this round = rage ends. ===
Switch monster back to endTurn so no damage is taken.
Monster endTurn.
Barbarian ends R3 turn without attacking or taking damage.
Monster endTurn. Between R3 end and this point rage-end check fires: rageAttackedThisTurn=false + rageDamageTakenThisTurn=false → rage expires.
=== R4: Attack WITHOUT rage (should deal unboosted damage). Then re-rage on same turn as bonus action (pool 2→1). ===
Damage 8 + 4 (STR) + 0 (NO rage) = 12. Golem 186→174 (rather than 186→172 if rage had persisted).
Rage expired confirmed: attack dealt 12 (not 14). 2-damage delta proves rage ended.
Re-enter Rage this same turn (bonus action). Pool 2→1.
Rage pool 2→1 after re-activation (R1 used the first, R4 uses the second). Pool correctly persists across rage end and re-use.
=== R4 attempt to rage again should error (already raging). ===
